description: Target curves required before the simulator can move from qualitative behavior-level ABM toward quantitative species-level biological calibration.

readiness_scale.missing_digitized_data: A paper or experiment is known, but no digitized numeric target curve is available in the repository.
readiness_scale.model_reference_only: A mathematical or qualitative literature model exists, but not digitized raw biological measurements.
readiness_scale.qualitative_proxy_only: A qualitative probe exists, but the curve needed for fitting has not been digitized.
readiness_scale.ready_for_fit: Digitized x/y target data with units and source metadata are available.

required_for_level_4: At least one ready_for_fit target for trail dynamics or foraging recruitment.
At least one independent validation curve not used for parameter fitting.
Species-specific unit mapping for time, distance and core physiological variables.

targets.biological_process: Independent no-jam traffic validation: density versus mean velocity on a natural ant trail.

source_candidates.title: Trafficlike collective movement of ants on trails: absence of a jammed phase

targets.current_proxy: paper_conditions_v5 exports no-jam speed and flow metrics; validate_traffic_holdout.py compares normalized speed retention without fitting.
targets.next_action: Use as independent holdout while fitting traffic/contact parameters to a separate curve.

targets.biological_process: Individual turning response as a function of local pheromone difference and total pheromone.

source_candidates.title: Individual rules for trail pattern formation in Argentine ants

required_data: turning slope or turn angle response
source figure/table and fitted parameter confidence interval
targets.current_proxy: paper_conditions_v5 exports per-step sensing and turning logs but previously lacked a numeric target curve.
targets.next_action: Use the Perna 2012 response curve to calibrate shared Weber-style turn-response parameters, then validate against an independent holdout curve.

targets.biological_process: Pheromone trail decay after food removal or rain/washout.

source_candidates.title: Decay rates of attractive and repellent pheromones in an ant foraging trail network
A continuous model of ant foraging with pheromones and trail formation
Individual rules for trail pattern formation in Argentine ants

required_data: time since food removal or washout
trail pheromone proxy or trail occupancy
targets.current_proxy: literature_pheromone_constraints_v1 checks qualitative persistence effects only.
targets.next_action: Digitize trail decay or trail occupancy time series and map model day to the paper's time axis.

targets.biological_process: Food quality or concentration versus recruitment/trail-laying strength.

source_candidates.title: Modulation of pheromone trail strength with food quality in Pharaoh's ant, Monomorium pharaonis

required_data: trail-laying event rate or recruitment count
targets.current_proxy: paper_conditions_v5 checks high-quality source bias qualitatively.
targets.next_action: Digitize food-quality recruitment curves and add a concentration-to-quality mapping.

targets.biological_process: Branch choice probability/time course under symmetric and biased trails.
targets.current_proxy: calibrate_double_bridge.py fits a literature model reference, not raw biological counts.
targets.next_action: Replace or supplement the model reference with digitized branch-choice curves from a real double-bridge experiment.

targets.biological_process: Trail density versus speed/flow under crowding.

source_candidates.title: Optimal traffic organisation in ants under crowded conditions
Trafficlike collective movement of ants on trails: absence of a jammed phase

targets.next_action: Digitize speed-density or flow-density curves and fit contact/crowding parameters.

targets.biological_process: Frontal encounter pushing/redirect probability under crowded ant traffic.

source_candidates.title: Optimal traffic organisation in ants under crowded conditions

targets.current_proxy: paper_conditions_v5 now exports traffic_redirect_per_encounter from the shared traffic-pressure rule; validate_pushing_redirect.py compares it to the Figure 3d slope.
targets.next_action: Keep as a mechanism holdout while fitting traffic/contact parameters to independent traffic curves.

targets.biological_process: Temperature/humidity stress versus brood survival or development.

source_candidates.title: Plastic collective endothermy in army ant bivouacs

targets.next_action: Digitize brood survival/development curves or collect lab data for the target species.

targets.biological_process: Worker survival benefit of free corpse removal versus restricted corpse removal.

source_candidates.title: Keep the nest clean: survival advantages of corpse removal in ants

targets.current_proxy: paper_conditions_v5 now exports free-removal and restricted-removal necrophoresis survival conditions using the shared nest-corpse social-immunity pressure rule.
targets.next_action: Use the endpoint as an independent social-immunity holdout, then fit raw survival time-series from the supplementary data.

targets.biological_process: Queen founding reserves, egg laying and first worker emergence over time.
targets.current_proxy: actual_biology_simulation.py has founding_colony viability smoke output only.
targets.next_action: Select a target species and add founding-stage laboratory or literature count curves.
